title: ChangeSpec Event
description: A ChangeSpec 1.0 event describing a software change from a vendor or package maintainer.

id.description: Globally unique event identifier. Producers SHOULD use the format 'cs_' followed by a ULID or UUIDv4.

vendor_id.description: Identifies the vendor or package. Optionally namespace-prefixed (e.g., 'npm:lodash', 'pypi:requests'). No-namespace slugs identify named services.

category.description: Change category classifying the type of change.

severity.description: Change severity indicating urgency and potential impact.

title.description: Short human-readable change title. No newlines. No markdown.

summary.description: 1-5 sentence plain-English description of what changed and the impact.

published_at.description: RFC 3339 datetime when this event was published.

source_type.description: How this event was produced. publisher_verified: vendor pushed directly. crawled: automated detection. community: community submission.

effective_date.description: RFC 3339 full-date (YYYY-MM-DD) when the change takes effect.

source_url.description: URL of the primary source document for this event. Must use the https:// scheme.

affected_versions.description: Semver range (npm syntax) describing which versions are affected. Example: '>=14.0.0 <15.0.0'.

fixed_in_version.description: Exact semver version where this issue is resolved.

migration_hint.description: One or two sentences describing the immediate action a consumer should take. Plain text.

migration_url.description: URL of a migration guide or detailed remediation instructions. Must use the https:// scheme.

confidence_score.description: Producer confidence in the classification. 1.0 for publisher_verified. Lower values for automated classification.

sunset_date.description: RFC 3339 full-date when the deprecated feature is removed or new terms take effect.

cve_id.description: CVE identifier. For security events only. Example: CVE-2024-12345.

cvss_score.description: CVSS base score (0.0-10.0). For security events only.

affected_systems.description: Named systems or services within the vendor that are affected.

affected_sections.description: Document section headers affected (relevant for TOS/DPA changes).

action_required.description: Whether consumers must take action. True for breaking changes and critical security events.

recommended_reviewers.description: Suggested reviewer roles. Values are advisory.

signature.description: Ed25519 signature block. Present only on publisher_verified events.

key_id.description: Identifies the signing key for public key lookup. Scoped by vendor_id at resolution time.

value.description: Base64url-encoded Ed25519 signature (64 bytes, no padding).

signed_fields.description: Ordered list of field names included in the signature input. Must include all required event fields plus signature.alg, signature.key_id, and signature.signed_fields.

key_fingerprint.description: Optional SHA-256 fingerprint of the public key, base64url-encoded (43 characters, no padding). When present, consumers MUST verify the resolved key matches this fingerprint.

examples.title: confirm() now requires return_url parameter
examples.summary: The PaymentIntent confirm() method now requires the return_url parameter in all cases where a redirect may occur. Calls without return_url that previously succeeded will now return a 400 error.
examples.migration_hint: Add return_url to all confirm() calls.
